description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: test and build. In the test stage add three ASAN jobs—ASAN-fix-volk (opensuse/tumbleweed), ASAN-gcc13 (ubuntu:mantic) and ASAN-gcc7 (ubuntu:bionic)—each allowing failure and set to manual; they run a series of commands that update the package manager, install build dependencies (zypper or apt), clone and build VOLK and GNURadio with cmake and make (including custom sed patch in ASAN-fix-volk), set NPROC=$(grep -c ^processor /proc/cpuinfo), compile with make -j$(($NPROC+1)), install, copy a test configuration file from /captures/gitlab_test_signals/static, and execute gnss-sdr with that config. In the build stage add archlinux (archlinux:latest) which runs pacman-key init, updates keys, installs gcc, make, cmake, boost, gnuradio, etc., builds with cmake, runs make, make check, make install, runs volk_gnsssdr_profile and cleans up; centos9 (quay.io/centos/centos:stream9) which installs epel, development tools, clones and builds libiio, libad9361-iio, VOLK, GNURadio, BLAS, LAPACK, then builds GNURadio with many -DENABLE flags, runs benchmarks and installs; coverity-scan (ubuntu:noble) which installs wget and curl, downloads the Coverity tool using $COVERITY_SCAN_TOKEN, verifies MD5, extracts it, adds it to PATH, installs build dependencies, configures GNURadio with cmake, runs cov-build, packages the results into gnss-sdr.tgz, and uploads it via curl, storing the artifact gnss-sdr.tgz; crosscompile (ubuntu:xenial) which installs build tools, downloads and verifies an ARM cross‑toolchain script, runs it, sources the environment, configures GNURadio with a custom toolchain file and many -DENABLE options, builds, installs to a local directory, removes unwanted binaries, copies the resulting binaries and share files into a binaries/ folder and archives them as artifacts; and debian-sid (debian:sid) which updates, upgrades, and installs build‑essential, pkg-config, cmake, git and other dependencies (script truncated). All jobs specify appropriate tags (gauss, kepler), allow_failure where needed, manual triggers for ASAN jobs and coverity‑scan, and define artifacts for coverity‑scan and crosscompile.
